profile.name: automation
profile.intent: Operate device, storage, network, and remote-runtime work — SSH/HPC/RunAI coordination, scheduler-backed job probes, credential boundaries, and routine automations — independent of the ML research paper lifecycle.

routing.handoff_policy: Enter through project-ops-router, then hand off to remote-project-control for SSH/HPC/RunAI work or run-status-monitor for scheduler/process status probes. Depends on core-ops for shared git/memory/docs/sidecar substrate; do not duplicate core-ops skills here.

install_policy.notes: Install alongside core-ops when a folder does remote/runtime/ops work without the full ML research lifecycle (e.g., cluster maintenance, remote experiment monitoring, storage cleanup). Do not auto-install ML lifecycle skills.

workflow_contract.lane_selection_rule: Use combined for durable remote ops because mutation should follow state inspection; use action for bounded scheduled tasks, evidence for status audits, and no-contract only for transient read-only probes.

required_preflight: Detect remote target (SSH host, HPC scheduler, RunAI namespace) and authentication state before any command.
Read project conventions for remote wrapper scripts and forbidden remote command shapes.
Classify whether the action is read-only status probe, scheduler submission, or destructive cleanup before executing.
forbidden_actions: Do not run destructive remote cleanup without explicit approval and a rollback record.
Do not embed credentials or auth tokens in repo-tracked artifacts.
Do not copy raw remote SSH output or scheduler dumps into public memory or docs.
action_lane.approval_boundary: Escalate before destructive remote operations, credential rotation, scheduler-wide kills, network/firewall changes, or any operation that touches shared infrastructure beyond a single user namespace.

evidence_lane.confidence_policy: Treat live remote command output as execution truth and status artifacts as durable summaries; never substitute one for the other when escalating.
evidence_lane.citation_policy: Cite remote command names, scheduler IDs, and status artifact paths; do not paste raw transcripts into public docs.

combined_lane.handoff_artifact: status artifact under docs/ops/runs/, rollback record, or remote-command transcript summary
combined_lane.staleness_trigger: Scheduler state changes, new job submissions, credential rotation, or remote wrapper policy changes.

no_contract_lane.allowed_when: The probe is read-only, transient, does not write status artifacts or memory, and does not modify remote or local files.
no_contract_lane.escalation_trigger: The task requires scheduler submission, credential access, remote mutation, status artifact persistence, or network changes.

acceptance_checks: Kernel example references the automation profile and project-ops-router entrypoint.
Action lane names remote, scheduler, credentialed, and network mutation surfaces with explicit approval boundary.
No ML paper lifecycle or experiment-design skills are required by default; depends on core-ops for shared substrate.

privacy_boundary.publication_audit_required_when: Use memory-publication-auditor before turning private remote-ops lessons, raw transcripts, or local cluster paths into public automation rules.

selection_contexts: Select automation directly for remote-ops, scheduler-status, cluster-maintenance, or storage/network-management work without ML paper lifecycle context.
Pair automation with core-ops when the folder also needs git, memory, docs, or sidecar substrate.
Do not require automation to beat the ML research router in a full ML project context; ml-research absorbs automation skills there.
selection_semantics.delegation_policy: After selection, delegate to remote-project-control for SSH/HPC/RunAI work, run-status-monitor for scheduler/process probes, or sidecar-task-runner for bounded delegated subtasks. Depend on core-ops for git/memory/docs/sidecar substrate rather than reimplementing it.
selection_semantics.installable_manifest_gate: Installable manifests should preserve remote/runtime/ops selection before leaf-skill routing and keep ML paper lifecycle skills out of the default route.

promotion_gates: Remote-ops and scheduler-probe skills can be installed without pulling ML paper lifecycle skills.
Credential boundary, status artifact format, and remote wrapper policy remain coherent after extraction.
Runtime adapters can be generated without duplicating source logic into Codex or Claude Code metadata.
Depends-on-core-ops relationship is documented and respected by installers and routers.
rejection_checks: Do not extract if automation still depends on ML-specific claims, experiment semantics, or paper-writing skills.
Do not make credentials, auth tokens, or local cluster paths part of public kernel examples.
Do not split before the core-ops dependency relationship is recorded in profile-index and adapter docs.
